item.title:
IRAN SETS HARD LINE ON HORMUZ TALKS
Iran’s First Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref said negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz will only happen if adversaries pledge not to invade and recognize Iran’s international rights.
Echoing combative rhetoric, he added that Iran’s opponents are now “begging” for talks after the waterway was effectively shut to commercial shipping.
item.description:
IRAN SETS HARD LINE ON HORMUZ TALKS

Iran’s First Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref said negotiations over the Strait of Hormuz will only happen if adversaries pledge not to invade and recognize Iran’s international rights.

Echoing combative rhetoric, he added that Iran’s opponents are now “begging” for talks after the waterway was effectively shut to commercial shipping.

item.title:
Pinned: RECESSION ODDS SURGE TO 40%
Prediction markets now price a 40% chance of a recession this year — a sharp climb over recent weeks. https://kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnber/recession/kxrecssnber-26?utm_source=walterbloomberg
item.description:
RECESSION ODDS SURGE TO 40%

Prediction markets now price a 40% chance of a recession this year — a sharp climb over recent weeks.
kalshi.com/markets/kxrecssnb…

item.title:
OIL CRISIS PUSHES BRENT TO $125
Société Générale has raised its Brent forecast, expecting prices to average $125 per barrel in April as the Strait of Hormuz disruption continues into next month.
Analysts say flows through Hormuz remain at crisis levels, leaving a roughly 15 million barrels per day gap in global supply. The region is largely stalled, with only limited rerouting by Saudi Arabia and the UAE, and occasional Iranian shipments.
item.description:
OIL CRISIS PUSHES BRENT TO $125

Société Générale has raised its Brent forecast, expecting prices to average $125 per barrel in April as the Strait of Hormuz disruption continues into next month.

Analysts say flows through Hormuz remain at crisis levels, leaving a roughly 15 million barrels per day gap in global supply. The region is largely stalled, with only limited rerouting by Saudi Arabia and the UAE, and occasional Iranian shipments.
